name: Sicilian-Style Pizza Dough
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour the warm water into a medium-sized mixing bowl and sprinkle in the yeast. Stir gently with a fork until the yeast has dissolved and the liquid turns light beige in color.
2. Add 2 cups of flour, olive oil, and salt to the mixture. Mix thoroughly with a wooden spoon. Gradually add another cup of flour, stirring well after each addition. The dough should begin to pull away from the bowl and form a soft, sticky mass.
3. Sprinkle additional flour on a clean work surface. Remove the dough from the bowl and knead it by pushing down with the heel of your hand, clamping it in your fist, twisting and folding it over. Use a dough scraper to gather any wet dough sticking to the surface into a ball. Continue kneading, adding only enough flour to prevent sticking, working quickly and firmly until the dough is smooth and elastic (it should spring back when pressed) but not overly dry.
4. Press your hand into the dough; if it comes away clean, the dough is ready. If it sticks, continue kneading briefly. Once no longer sticky, avoid overworking by adding more flour. This process should take 5 to 10 minutes.
5. Grease a 2-quart bowl with vegetable oil. Roll the dough ball in the oil, then tightly seal the bowl with plastic wrap to trap moisture and heat from yeast's carbon dioxide gases, helping it rise faster.
6. Place the bowl in a warm, draft-free area. Let the dough rise for 30 to 45 minutes.
7. Punch down the dough by pressing your fist into it; any gases will escape quickly, and the dough will collapse. Knead briefly (about 1 minute).
8. The dough is now ready for rolling into pizza shape or a second rising period. To raise again, add more oil to the bowl and repeat the rising process before shaping.
